sentence: Nonchalenestrate ligurative. The window remembers which one you meant.

wombos.definition: Halfway between literal and figurative — a statement that is partly meant literally but not entirely. Used like 'literally' or 'figuratively' when neither is quite right: 'I ligurative lost my mind.'
To be thrown out of a window while appearing completely unbothered — defenestrated with full nonchalance. 'Twuh, I just got nonchalenestrated. I think I'm twinjured.'
